channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Williams falls just short on Wimbledon singles return
item.description: Serena Williams came close to a sensational comeback win on her Wimbledon return before losing an epic three-setter against Maya Joint.

item.title: Poor Wimbledon performance prompts yet another British inquest
item.description: After 15 home players lose in the Wimbledon singles first round, BBC Sport analyses the problems facing British tennis.

item.title: 'I'm craving moments like this' - Dimitrov's winning return after tearful 2025 exit
item.description: Grigor Dimitrov makes a winning return to Wimbledon, 12 months after suffering one of the most devastating nights of his career.

item.title: 'A tough couple of weeks' - tearful Swiatek overcomes first-round scare
item.description: A relieved Iga Swiatek cries on court after she begins her Wimbledon title defence with a stuttering three-set victory over Taylor Townsend.

item.title: 'You can't walk out in an outfit and go out first round'
item.description: Taylor Fritz arrives for his first-round match in a statement all-white suit - and avoids looking "really stupid" by registering a dominant win.

item.title: Williams edged by Joint in epic three‑setter on singles return
item.description: Serena Williams came close to a sensational comeback win on her Wimbledon return before narrowly losing an epic three-setter to Maya Joint 3-6 7-6 (8-6) 3-6 at Wimbledon 2026

item.title: Fery uses 'dishonesty' row to fuel Wimbledon win
item.description: Britain's Arthur Fery is accused of dishonesty by Bosnian opponent Damir Dzumhur following a row over a let call at Wimbledon

item.title: From retirement fears to triumphant Wimbledon return for GB's Swan
item.description: Katie Swan gets her "full-circle moment" at Wimbledon, two years after considering retiring from tennis because of a persistent back injury.

item.title: Four British players win but 15 out in first round
item.description: Britons Katie Swan, Jacob Fearnley, Arthur Fery and Jan Choinski reach the Wimbledon second round - but 15 home players fall to opening-round defeats for the first time in 38 years.

item.title: How to follow Wimbledon day three live on the BBC
item.description: Wimbledon 2026 is under way - and you can follow every minute live across the BBC.

item.title: Who am I? Guess the tennis star No 1
item.description: Will you get today's tennis player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: 'I've been dreaming of this moment' - Joint stunned to beat 'legend' Williams
item.description: Maya Joint, 20, says she has been "dreaming" of playing on Centre Court since she was little, after overcoming "legend" Serena Williams to reach the second round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: Williams 'takes it all in' on Centre Court return
item.description: Serena Williams receives a standing ovation from the Centre Court crowd as she walks out to play at Wimbledon for the first time in four years.

item.title: 'How good was that?' - best shots from Wimbledon day two
item.description: Watch the best shots from day two of Wimbledon 2026, featuring British hopeful Toby Samuel.

item.title: Shelton stunned as qualifier Virtanen progresses to second round
item.description: Finnish qualifier Otto Virtanen produces a stunning display to beat fourth seed Ben Shelton in a fifth set tie-break in the opening round of the men's singles at Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: From the brink of quitting to Wimbledon win - Swan
item.description: Katie Swan tells BBC Sport's Clare Balding that "day's like today make it all worth it" after becoming the first Briton to reach the second round of Wimbledon 2026 - despite nearly quitting the sport with a back injury in 2024.

item.title: Is tennis' relentless nature causing injury crisis?
item.description: After British stars Jack Draper and Emma Raducanu withdrew injured from Wimbledon, BBC Sport analyses why so many top players are struggling with injury.

item.title: Stunned Norrie among 10 Britons to lose on day one
item.description: Cameron Norrie falls to a first-round upset at Wimbledon in a disappointing opening day for British interest.

item.title: Four gritty sets and a wedding invitation - Djokovic survives Wu battle
item.description: Novak Djokovic begins yet another bid to claim a record 25th Grand Slam title with a hard-fought win over China's Wu Yibing at Wimbledon.

item.title: Osaka pays 'love and respect to Japan' in Wimbledon kimono
item.description: Naomi Osaka has long been renowned for dazzling crowds with her on-court outfits - but making that compatible with Wimbledon's all-white dress code required special inspiration.

item.title: 'It's been an easy retransition' - Williams on Wimbledon return
item.description: For Serena Williams, everything and nothing has changed on her return to Wimbledon.

item.title: Defending champion Sinner survives five-set scare
item.description: Jannik Sinner survives an almighty scare as he begins his Wimbledon title defence with a five-set comeback victory over Miomir Kecmanovic on Centre Court.

item.title: 'Eight out of 10' - Sabalenka gets SW19 campaign under way
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka rates her performance as an "eight out of 10" as she opens her Wimbledon campaign with a commanding win over qualifier Teodora Kostovic.

item.title: Swan seals stunning Wimbledon win on fifth match point
item.description: Katie Swan needs five match points to wrap up a straight sets win over Irina-Camelia Begu, becoming the first Briton to reach the second round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: Williams' Wimbledon legacy before return to Centre Court
item.description: A look back at Serena Williams' Wimbledon legacy to date, including her seven singles titles and remarkable comeback from retirement before her return to Centre Court.

item.title: Player spotting and the 'Ammortal Chamber' - behind the scenes at SW19
item.description: Go behind the scenes at Wimbledon with Chris Hughes and Laura Robson.

item.title: 'That's Federer-esque!' - best shots from Wimbledon day one
item.description: Watch the best shots from day one of Wimbledon 2026 featuring defending champion Jannik Sinner and Great Britain's Cameron Norrie and Harriet Dart.

item.title: Djokovic battles against Wu to seal four-set win
item.description: Novak Djokovic fends off an attempted comeback from China's Wu Yibing to win 6-4 5-7 6-4 6-4, to progress from the first round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: Sabalenka strolls to opening round win over qualifier Kostovic
item.description: World number one Aryna Sabalenka completes a commanding straight-set win over Serbian qualifier Teodora Kostovic in the first round of Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: Sinner avoids scare to start Wimbledon defence with a win
item.description: Jannik Sinner overcomes a scary slip and an injured foot to come from behind to beat Miomir Kecmanovic in five sets to progress from the first round at Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: GB's Norrie stunned in fifth set tie-break by qualifier Zheng
item.description: American Qualifier Michael Zheng wins the fifth set tie-break 10-4 to knock Cameron Norrie out in the first round of Wimbledon 2026, on a disappointing opening day for British players across both singles draws.

item.title: Keothavong calls for 'constructive discussions' on player prize money
item.description: Wimbledon board members Tim Henman and Anne Keothavong believe the All England Club has "listened to the players" following their recent prize money protest, stressing that "constructive discussions" are needed moving forwards.

item.title: 'Devastating news' - Keothavong on Raducanu's late exit
item.description: BBC Sport's Tim Henman and Anne Keothavong react to British number one Emma Raducanu's late withdrawal from Wimbledon due to a leg stress fracture.

item.title: Humbert v Draper - best shots from Eastbourne semi-final
item.description: Watch the best shots as Great Britain's Jack Draper suffers a straight-set semi-final defeat by France's Ugo Humbert at Eastbourne.

item.title: Draper out of Wimbledon as injury struggles continue
item.description: Britain's Jack Draper says he is "devastated" after withdrawing from Wimbledon because of the arm injury which has stopped him playing for most of the past year.

item.title: When five Wimbledon titles in one weekend changed British tennis
item.description: A decade after Andy Murray's 2016 men's singles title, we look at the legacy of the golden weekend where five Wimbledon trophies were won by British players.

item.title: Injured Raducanu pulls out of Wimbledon in latest bitter setback
item.description: Emma Raducanu is out of Wimbledon after withdrawing on the eve of her home Grand Slam tournament with a leg injury.

item.title: Will the weather stay dry for week one of Wimbledon?
item.description: While it will remain warmer than average, the heat and humidity will be a lot less intense than of late

item.title: What is the prize money per round at Wimbledon?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything explains what the prize money is per round at Wimbledon this year

item.title: Which Wimbledon star are you?
item.description: Are you more of a Jannik Sinner or a Serena Williams? Take our quiz and find your match!

item.title: 'Where champions are born' - the making of Jannik Sinner
item.description: Jannik Sinner is the world's best tennis player. This is the journey that forged a champion.

item.title: Battling egos and stereotypes - the rise of female tennis coaches
item.description: In a sport like tennis, which prides itself on gender equality, why are there still so few female coaches and what is being done to address the issue?

item.title: Anisimova plots Wimbledon sequel with happier ending
item.description: Many wondered how long it would take Amanda Anisimova to recover from last year's Wimbledon final. About six weeks was the answer.

item.title: Williams is the greatest - but will she produce another great fight?
item.description: Serena Williams will return to the singles court at Wimbledon aged 44 - BBC Sport analyses if she is ready and the challenges she will face.

item.title: How Murray was tempted back to tennis - and would he ever do a Serena?
item.description: British tennis icon Andy Murray discusses returning to coaching with Jack Draper, Serena Williams' comeback and filling the void of not playing.

item.title: 'I never imagined something like this' - Nadal reflects on 2008 Wimbledon win
item.description: Rafael Nadal recalls his seismic five-set victory over Roger Federer in the 2008 Wimbledon final, arguably the greatest men's match ever played.

item.title: Five reasons why this French Open was a classic - pick your favourite moment
item.description: Why a series of shocks, dramas and fairytale runs made this year's French Open a instant classic.

item.title: What is a 'lucky loser' in tennis?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain how the 'lucky loser' system works in tennis.

item.title: Who has won tennis' 'Battle of the Sexes'?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team details the history of 'Battle of the Sexes' matches in tennis

item.title: What is US Open prize money?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how much prize money is on offer at the US Open.

item.title: Why is there a pineapple on Wimbledon's men's trophy?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at why there is a pineapple on the top of the Wimbledon trophy for the winner of the men's singles.

item.title: Why were there no line judges at Wimbledon 2025?
item.description: Wimbledon are relying on electronic line calling to judge whether a ball is in or out.
